characters.physical_details: Tall, rangy, dark-haired; nicotine-yellowed fingers, paint under the nails, scar under the left ribs. Right wrist is sprained in chapter 8; right hand is singed in chapter 17.
characters.occupation_status: Commercial sign painter and namer until the chapter 14 Patent Row eviction; afterward ward laborer and co-maker of shared route boards with Inez.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English; can letter limited Spanish and trade French.

state_transitions.change: Signs Gideon Wren's exclusivity retainer for the runaway moss.
Exposed for stealing ward names, cast out of Sump Ward, and sprains his right wrist.
Becomes Inez's lover and is allowed to read her private ledger.
Publicly gives Celadon Crown under coercion tied to Junie's fuel debt.
Loses his Patent Row shop.
Begins ward labor and sketches the first multi-name public board.
Burns the contract, singes his right hand, and accepts no-sole-credit shared signage.
By early autumn shares a room above Junie's kitchen with Inez and works ferry and stair boards.

characters.availability: Present throughout; precision is limited after chapter 8.

characters.physical_details: Compact, strong-shouldered, sure-footed on wet planks; dark hair usually under a work scarf. Old crescent burn scar inside the right wrist.
characters.occupation_status: Sump Ward lamplight keeper, moss grower, and practical naming keeper; later co-runs route boards and the jobs ledger.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes English and Spanish; keeps mixed practical field notes.

state_transitions.change: Allows provisional collaboration with Silas.
Becomes Silas's lover and shares the ledger.
Breaks from Silas after the coerced regatta christening.
Leads the Bellglass chorus-board action.
Sets no-sole-credit and return-path rules; opens the shared-labor ledger with Silas.
Shares a room above Junie's kitchen with Silas and starts regular ferry-and-stair board work.

characters.physical_details: Lean, silvering at the temples, carefully dressed, usually gloved in public; unnaturally clean for the city.
characters.occupation_status: Head of Verdancy House, using print, debt purchase, depot rights, pamphlets, and distribution to standardize names; after Bellglass he shifts to licensed variants and approved-name lists.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in English and business French.

state_transitions.change: Retains Silas under exclusivity.
Uses Junie's debt paper to force the public Celadon Crown regatta christening.
Loses single-name dominance at Bellglass Bridge.
Loses Silas and has Verdancy's debt-harvest and language-extraction papers exposed.

characters.availability: Appears with Verdancy pressure and spectacle.

characters.physical_details: Broad-shouldered and quick-handed, usually flour- or broth-marked; hair pinned up for work; old healed split in the left eyebrow.
characters.occupation_status: Runs a Ferrymouth kitchen for ferry crews and haulers; by the ending it is also a correction point for route boards, cuttings, and barter.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads and writes plain English for tabs, recipes, and accounts.

state_transitions.change: Reveals rising rent and fuel pressure at Ferrymouth.
Her fuel notes become Gideon's leverage over Silas.
Forced onto weak branded gold fuel and thinner margins.
Gets her notes back through Damaris's broadside and joins the shared-board arrangement.
Runs a three-hand kitchen board and trades warnings, jars, food, and fuel through Ferrymouth routes.

characters.physical_details: Short, sturdy, broad-handed, thick white braid; hands stained by heat and algae; one eye drifts when she is tired or amused.
characters.occupation_status: Senior grow-house worker and keeper of Mothers' Drain memory, mother stock, and practical naming.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads devotional print slowly; writes names and measurements in a work hand; speaks English mixed with river Spanish.

state_transitions.change: Publicly condemns Silas at the Feast of Mud Names.
Reveals the Mothers' Drain origin of the strain and the surviving communal mother tray.
Frames the redundancy tactic: many surfaces, many keepers, no single sainted owner.

characters.physical_details: Wears widow's black gone shiny at the seams; ink on her fingers; chipped front tooth she does not hide.
characters.occupation_status: Editor-proprietor of a scandal sheet on Patent Row and the ferries.
characters.literacy_languages: Highly literate in English; fast editor and proofreader.

state_transitions.change: Turns the runaway moss and Silas into serialized copy.
Gets Silas into Gideon's regatta preview and reveals the saturation plan.
Commits ruined newsprint, runners, and print circulation to the Bellglass action.
Publishes Verdancy's debt-harvest and extraction materials.

characters.availability: Present when rumor or print matter.

characters.physical_details: Freckled, narrow-shouldered, usually in hand-me-downs; one knee stays dock-dirty; front tooth missing through the middle chapters and growing back crooked by chapter 18.

key_relationships.inez_cordero: adult he obeys when the work is real

characters.occupation_status: Schoolboy who helps at Ferrymouth and later carries hand-copies, small boards, and warning slats.
characters.literacy_languages: Learns schoolroom reading and sounding-out; reads posted words well enough to spot errors.

state_transitions.change: Begins visibly trying on public names in everyday speech.
Repeats branded Celadon language from school and household traffic.
Helps distribute hand-copies and small boards during the Bellglass action.
Rides work ferries, copies warning slats, and tests shared names without claiming ownership.

characters.availability: Present in family scenes and later route work.

timeline.story_start: Late spring 1898, after high water recedes and humid digester weather returns to Nacre Bend.
timeline.estimated_span: Roughly four months, late spring to early autumn 1898; about one month passes between Bellglass and chapter 18.

key_events.event: Inez shows the household harm of public naming.
Silas signs Gideon's retainer as Junie's debt pressure sharpens.
The first Celadon Crown launch collapses.
Silas is exposed for stealing ward names and injures his wrist.
Mothers' Drain lineage and Gideon's regatta plan are uncovered.
The coalition chooses distributed boards and return paths.
The coerced regatta christening drives the pale-gold phase.
Silas loses his Patent Row shop.
The contract burns, Verdancy papers print, and the jobs ledger begins.
By early autumn, shared route boards run Ferrymouth to Lantern Steps while pamphlets still compete.

geography.primary_setting: Nacre Bend, an 1898 river city of terraces, ferry slips, low flood wards, and algae-fueled light.

key_locations.name: Patent Row
key_locations.details: Commercial corridor of sign shops, papers, patent stalls, and repainted wall space.

key_locations.name: Sump Ward
key_locations.details: Low communal district of grow houses, plank walks, wash lines, and households that need practical mosslight.

key_locations.name: Ferrymouth Landing
key_locations.details: Dock district around Junie's kitchen, ferry slips, cargo sheds, and the kitchen board exchange.

key_locations.name: Lantern Steps
key_locations.details: Steep stair district where names change by landing, weather, and use; lower landings become a public correction site in chapter 18.

key_locations.name: Gilt Crescent
key_locations.details: Ridge district of investors, showrooms, preview boathouses, and cleaner public surfaces.

key_locations.name: Bellglass Bridge
key_locations.details: Visible iron choke point over the drainage cut; the chapter 16 plurality break happens here.

key_locations.name: Mothers' Drain
key_locations.details: Unmapped cut behind the Sump Ward wash houses where the communal mother line was bred after the old flood year.

distances: Patent Row to Ferrymouth is about a fifteen-minute walk in dry weather and much slower when gutters back up.
Sump Ward lies lower and wetter than Lantern Steps; locals move safely on narrow planks outsiders misread.
Gilt Crescent overlooks Patent Row and profits from light changes before it smells the ferment below.
Bellglass sits between Patent Row traffic and Sump Ward runoff, making it a natural public naming choke point.
The south ferry line links Ferrymouth and Lower Lantern Steps and becomes a fast warning-board route by chapter 18.
Mothers' Drain runs behind the Sump Ward wash houses and underlies the communal mother-tray lineage Vero protects.

world_rules: Household light, cook heat, and some small engines run on algae mash and moss; fuel shortages immediately alter eating, work, and night travel.
A small family of chorus strains is lex-sensitive: repeated public naming can shift color, brightness, heat, and minor behavior, but cannot create matter.
The effect strengthens through street cries, signs, songs, papers, ferry talk, and repeated household speech.
Competing names can coexist by district, weather, or use; one citywide name requires ongoing saturation, not a single declaration.
Practical names carry use, danger, and handling obligations; stripping that context harms people even when the moss still glows.
Printed words matter only when they are read, repeated, corrected, or posted in public use.
Heat, humidity, and fermentation accelerate shifts; early-autumn cooling slows them without ending them.
Distributed boards and local correction can hold multiple names at once; no official list stays authoritative without keepers behind it.

power_structure.over: small inventors, hired namers, packet sellers, depot sellers, and markets they can flood
power_structure.mechanism: retainers, debt purchase, print, depot rights, pamphlets, and saturation campaigns

power_structure.holder: landlords and fuel creditors such as Aram Pell
power_structure.over: Ferrymouth tenants, kitchens, and storage access
power_structure.mechanism: rent pressure, arrears, fuel lines, and padlock threats

power_structure.holder: Aunt Vero and Sump Ward grow-house matrons
power_structure.over: mother stock, cuttings, practical instruction, and communal memory
power_structure.mechanism: expert knowledge, reciprocal obligation, and daily maintenance

power_structure.over: public rumor and the speed with which a local fight becomes citywide

power_structure.holder: route keepers such as Junie, Captain Lusk, wash-house women, boardinghouse keepers, hawkers, and children
power_structure.over: which practical names survive in daily use
power_structure.mechanism: high-traffic surfaces, oral correction, repeated practice, and refusal to let one district own every use

objects.name: Inez's oilskin name ledger
objects.origin: Inherited from her mother and expanded through grow-house work; records names, uses, warnings, and lineage.
objects.status: Private until chapter 10, then shared with Silas; still kept by Inez.

objects.name: Verdancy exclusivity contract
objects.origin: Retainer signed by Silas in chapter 3 in exchange for an advance on stabilizing the runaway moss.
objects.status: Active from chapter 3 until Silas burns it in chapter 17.

objects.name: Sump Ward mother tray
objects.owner: aunt_vero_sanz as keeper for the communal houses
objects.origin: Surviving communal mother stock bred from Mothers' Drain by ward women after the old flood year.
objects.status: Hidden working proof from chapter 11 onward; used for splittings, not private title claims.

objects.name: Junie's fuel notes
objects.origin: Ferrymouth kitchen debt papers for fuel deliveries and arrears.
objects.status: Bought by Gideon before chapter 13 for leverage, then recovered and printed in chapter 17.

objects.name: Bellglass chorus boards and hand-copies
objects.owner: coalition around inez_cordero, junie_vale, and damaris_pike
objects.origin: Salvaged planks, ruined newsprint, paste, and hand-copies prepared for Bellglass.
objects.status: Hung, torn down, copied, and re-posted in chapter 16; public, revisable, and unsigned.

objects.name: Ferrymouth kitchen board
objects.owner: junie_vale with shared labor from silas_vale and inez_cordero
objects.origin: First durable board made under the no-sole-credit rules and entered in the jobs ledger.
objects.status: Hanging over Junie's pot shelf by chapter 18, updated in three hands with tagged cuttings below it.

objects.name: Shared labor jobs ledger
objects.origin: Cheap ruled ledger opened in chapter 17 to track hours, materials, payments, and obligations.
objects.status: Active by early autumn as the bookkeeping for Silas and Inez's board work.

objects.name: Approved-name pamphlets
objects.origin: Post-Bellglass authority lists sold to visitors, investors, and 'serious householders.'
objects.status: Circulating by chapter 18 but publicly mocked, corrected, and never fully authoritative.

debts.creditor: Aram Pell and allied fuel suppliers
debts.status: Bought by Gideon for chapter 13 coercion; notes recovered and exposed in chapter 17, though fuel pressure remains
Shop lost and frontage reassigned to a Verdancy depot seller

income_sources.reliability: good early, damaged after chapter 8, gone after chapter 14
steady demand but vulnerable to fuel price shocks and naming shifts

income_sources.source: Inez's grow-house work and cuttings barter
Verdancy packet sales and branded campaigns
income_sources.reliability: strong while debt, print, and distribution hold; weaker after Bellglass and chapter 17 exposure

income_sources.source: Shared route boards, warning slats, and practical signage
income_sources.reliability: modest but growing by early autumn

knowledge_state.knows: Local glow-moss names encode use, danger, heat, and handling rather than sales copy.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Patent Row buyers and Silas until chapter_04

knowledge_state.knows: He signed Gideon's exclusivity retainer and is not neutral in the naming fight.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Inez and Sump Ward until chapter_10

knowledge_state.knows: Her kitchen debt and fuel exposure are severe enough to be weaponized.
The runaway line descends from communal Mothers' Drain stock and one mother tray still survives.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Gideon, Silas, and the wider city until chapter_11

knowledge_state.knows: The surviving mother tray cannot be used as sole-title proof without creating another monopoly claim.
Gideon's regatta is a saturation campaign using song, print, ferries, school copy, and free cuttings.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: the coalition until she shares it in chapter_11

knowledge_state.knows: The counterplan only works if names stay tied to place, warnings, keepers, and return obligations across many surfaces.
Verdancy has been buying household debts and stripping ward names for resale.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: the public until her chapter_17 broadside

environmental_constants: The city smells of sweet rot, yeast, lamp smoke, river mud, hot algae mash, and damp paper.
Humidity makes walls sweat, paper curl, wood slick, and clothing cling; early autumn does not end the damp.
Night light is colored rather than neutral; green, blue, pink, gold, and mixed bands change mood and safety.
Catwalks, ferry ramps, stairs, and flood planks stay slick; locals and outsiders move differently.
Fermentation and grow spaces stay hot, wet, and noisy.
The Sallow River is always audible through bells, hull knocks, gulls, rope slap, and dock shouts.
Public walls and boards carry layered print, chalk, scraped paint, and corrections; by the ending clean single-slogan surfaces are rare.
